pattern: Layering the walk by how many teleports have been spent lets a per-layer grid DP interleave with a value-ranked prefix-minimum that performs the free jumps to any cell of no greater height.
hints: Track the number of teleports already used as an extra dimension; within one layer you only ever move right or down paying the entered cell's value.

A teleport from a cell goes to any cell whose value is at most the current one for free, so to relax a whole layer at once you need, for each value, the cheapest reachable cell among all cells of value at least that.

Sort cells by value descending and take a running minimum of the layer's costs; that gives each cell the best cost it can teleport in with. Run the right/down DP on each of the k+1 layers and answer with the cheapest arrival at the corner across all layers.

edge_inputs.description: increasing grid where teleports do not help
high start value lets you teleport far cheaply
single useful teleport across a costly row

stress.description: moderate square grid with small values and a few teleports
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    s = max(2, min(40, size))
    grid = [[rng.randrange(0, 50) for _ in range(s)] for _ in range(s)]
    k = rng.randrange(0, 6)
    return [grid, k]
stress.note: kept under 40x40 so the exponential-free oracle stays fast
